title: Brent crude rises above $106, touches $107.38 mid-morning Europe; 18% weekly gain
snippet: Brent crude rises above $106 per barrel, touching $107.38 in mid-morning European trading. Represents approximately 18% weekly gain — largest since 2022 Russia invasion. WTI follows similar pattern. Rising prices driven by continued Hormuz blockade, IRGC fast-boat threats, Ghalibaf resignation uncertainty, and Hegseth's 'as long as it takes' language.
tag_reason: Oil continues rising from yesterday's $105 — crosses $106 with intraday high $107.38. 18% weekly gain is new milestone. Update to ongoing oil price coverage.
